contents: I don't know if this will help anyone or not, as it did me, but I would like to share this, just incase. I have been having symptoms of bladder & kidney spasms (so bad, I thought I still had stones), pain and pressure in my pelvic area, nocturia, hesitancy, urinary stream that starts, stops, starts, stops, retention (some urine coming out, some staying in my bladder). So I started taking 2 water pills at bedtime every night and rarely suffer anymore. Sometimes I have to take a water pill during the day also but it's just amazing how well it controls these problems. If anyone has trouble urinating, you may want to check into this, instead of assuming it's the IC and that your stuck with it. That may not be the case.

I'm glad you're feeling better. Water pills (diuretics) are effective for some conditions, but they don't usually help with IC --- anyone wanting to try this should first check with a physician. Donna

contents: angel451- did this help with the nocturia? My biggest problem is getting up in the night. I am barely surviving on no sleep. You symptoms sound similar to mine.

I agree with you on this one. What I wouldn't give to just go to bed at night and only have to get up to go to the bathroom a couple of times I am lucky in one respect. That is that I usually fall back asleep fast. If you have trouble getting back to sleep, IC can be really debilitating. If you have to get up from a sleepless night and go to work, boy is that hard! I have tried drinking more, I have tried drinking less, I have tried having a snack, having no snack, etc. It all seems the same. I remember when I was a kid, my mother would come into my room and practically drag me to the table for Sunday dinner! Wow. To be able to sleep like that again! I agree with another person here who said that they will never take feeling good for granted again. It's so true. Karen

I have been having symptoms of bladder & kidney spasms (so bad, I thought I still had stones), pain and pressure in my pelvic area, nocturia, hesitancy, urinary stream that starts, stops, starts, stops, retention (some urine coming out, some staying in my bladder). So I started taking 2 water pills at bedtime every night and rarely suffer anymore. Sometimes I have to take a water pill during the day also but it's just amazing how well it controls these problems. It's just crazy how we are all so very different!! Wow! But--I would never consider taking my diuretic before bedtime!! I am already up every hour or so to pee as it is! All I need is more urine in my bladder to get rid of of!! With that said, I do take a diuretic every day (Maxzide) for fluid retention in my bodily tissues, especially my feet, legs, fingers and face. (Mine is not so severe to require self-cath tho'...) As far as my hesitancy and abnormal stream is concerned, I have learned the best way to avoid that is to avoid a severe flare if at all possible! (Which is sometimes unavoidable for me even with diet, pain meds, etc...) But, as long as my pain stays in the low-moderate ranges, this is not a problem for me. If, however, the pain spikes any higher than moderate, then the frequency, hesitancy and abnormal stream are the first things I notice--besides the god-awful, through-the-roof pain, of course! But, hey!! If it works for you and your doc says its okay, then by all means do it!! Heck, I would hop on one foot, chew on a red-Twizzler and sing "Zippidy-Do-Da"--while wearing a banana suit--if it proved to help me!! I am very glad you are finding some relief from this horrible disease!! ~Beth

contents: Originally posted by kristinj angel451- did this help with the nocturia? My biggest problem is getting up in the night. I am barely surviving on no sleep. You symptoms sound similar to mine. Yes it stops the nocturia just as long as I take them before I go to sleep (I have to take 2 but your only supposed to take 1 at a time), but if I don't take them, thinking I can sleep without them, I'm up all hours of the night going to the bathroom. It was also hard just getting to sleep because of all that pressure in my urinary tract, it caused a never ending urge to pee when I'd lay down.

Originally posted by BT2008 It's just crazy how we are all so very different!! Wow! But--I would never consider taking my diuretic before bedtime!! I am already up every hour or so to pee as it is! All I need is more urine in my bladder to get rid of of!! But, hey!! If it works for you and your doc says its okay, then by all means do it!! Heck, I would hop on one foot, chew on a red-Twizzler and sing "Zippidy-Do-Da"--while wearing a banana suit--if it proved to help me!! I am very glad you are finding some relief from this horrible disease!! ~Beth Water pills do not make me pee more, if anything, it makes it less bothersome. So they make your freqency worse? I guess what they do for me is allow my bladder to fully empty like it should, otherwise my bladder stays about half full after I pee, and that just gives you the urge to go back again and again. LOL. Thanks.

I was told by my urologist that the water pills are supposed to cause you to pee more so he's like wondering where all the water is going. He put me on a prescription, and this one is making me pee more but it seems to help the pain longer than the other pill but it's annoying peeing more. But it's just the first day.

When I went to bed last night, my skin (muscles) below my shoulder were jumping repeadedly fast. Can't say it hurt, it just freeked me out a little bit. I have chronic pain in two places in my back below my shoulders and in my neck. My doctor started me on Dyazide yesterday. Made me pee like a horse. I had to pee more than usual, though that wasn't too bad, but it feels like a gallon of urine is coming out everytime I go to the bathroom. The over-the-counter water pill wasn't quite that strong. This pill has a warning that it can raise your body's potassium levels, or cause dehydration or mineral loss. Yesterday was the first time I took it but it was also the first time my muscles ever jumped out of control like that. I don't know if it's related or not. In a way, I don't think it could be (it would be too fast of a reaction) but all at the same time, I'm kind of scared of this pill.

I was put on a diuretic once and I took one pill, and it caused me such bad spasms, I was going to the bathroom non stop the entire day and my bladder was hurting me. Goes to show how we are all so different.

contents: This diuretic can raise your potassium. I have a list of side effects from the pharmacy but thanks. And I know all medications are not without side effects, but when one says it can be fatal, I don't like it. My urologist said this stuff would also prevent kidney stones, one of the serious side effects listed are kidney stones. I couldn't even imagine that happening again. The potassium, if left untreated, can be fatal. It can also cause arrhythmia's, acute kidney failure, pancreatitis, diabetes, liver enzyme abnormalties (some of the worst reactions). The medication is so much more relieving with the pain than the over counter diuretic, it lasts continuously, the other is just short term acting. But, a risk of death is not worth getting rid of some pain. I have never taken medication that could be fatal. I know the doctor was trying to help but I just don't think it's worth taking that risk. It says my Ibuprofen will interact with it. That can't be good. And it is so easy for me to get side effects to medications. I've had no potassium before, it causes the worst muscle cramping you could imagine. But I don't know what to watch out for with high potassium. Those twitches last night could not have been this medication (I'm being ridiculous), I probably have a pinched nerve somewhere. I guess I'd feel safer taking it if I knew what to watch out for. But it does say to have your blood mineral levels (potassium, kidney, liver function tests, cholesterol level) monitored periodically so I guess it would be okay if he actually does that. I hate to not listen to him and not take it but I kind of value my life.

Originally posted by lisabar36 I was put on a diuretic once and I took one pill, and it caused me such bad spasms, I was going to the bathroom non stop the entire day and my bladder was hurting me. Goes to show how we are all so different. I do this with medications but it's almost always the one's that make it extremely difficult to pee. A water pill may drive you insane, but it relaxes my bladder like Detrol LA would do for you. Detrol LA would drive me mad. Yea, we are all different.

When I have spasms (esp strong enough to cut off and open up the urine stream), I used to take Ditropan. It works but puts me in such a fog that I can't function. Now after some training with my physical therapist, my husband stretches some of my internal muscles and holds the stretch until they relax into it. I also do some bridge exercises with kegels at the top of the bridge. It helps me a lot now, although I needed my muscles to be trained through the PT for it to work.

Hi I just wanted to add, Donna you are correct, diuretics deplete potassium, thats why when on them they recomend you add some potassium to your diet. As a nurse, we always gave patients bananas daily that were on duiretics, to help give them back some potassium, and most had to take potassium supplements, depending on the dose of the diuretic and there potassium levels had to be checked regularly.

contents: Originally posted by lisabar36 Hi I just wanted to add, Donna you are correct, diuretics deplete potassium, thats why when on them they recomend you add some potassium to your diet. As a nurse, we always gave patients bananas daily that were on duiretics, to help give them back some potassium, and most had to take potassium supplements, depending on the dose of the diuretic and there potassium levels had to be checked regularly. Some diuretics delete potassium, some don't. This is a potassium-sparing diuretic that can raise your potassium. I guess your more at risk if you take potassium suppliments with it. The Ibuprofen I've been told to use for my neck pain, with the combination of this, can cause acute renal failure. My urologist should have checked what medications I was taking before prescribing this one. I'll give it the chance but if I don't start feeling better in the next couple of weeks, I'm throwing this stuff in the garbage. I don't know if it's the pill or my fibromyalgia. I've quickly lost 7 pounds, my body hurts all over, and I am so tired, the longest I managed to stay awake yesterday is 4 hours and that's because someone was visiting and I couldn't go back to sleep, now I'm staying nauseas, and my abdomen is killing me. I feel like I need hospitalized.
My nose is starting to get stuffy, my head hurts, I'm coughing, weezing. I guess it's just a cold dragging me down, LOL. But it hurts!

Where should renal pain be located? I have it on both sides, a little below & above where the abdomen bends like when doing a side stretch, into my back. My hips and bladder hurt a little but nothing like that. I was screeming it got so bad. I felt urine in my kidneys again today. I sware there ought to be some type of IC disorder for someone's kidneys. Who ever feels urine in their side? I want to go to the hospital but last time I was there, they said they do not treat chronic pain. That's really messed up.

I hate you bladder! I checked my urine output after 45 minutes, I got half an ounce out of the 16ounces I drank. How do you still have bladder retention when your taking a water pill? Wonder if I was to avoid drinking anything during these bad episodes, if it would ease up the pain? Then there wouldn't be anything in me to cause all that pressure, at least.

<How do you still have bladder retention when your taking a water pill?> I know the answer to this (lol), it happens when your developing a UTI. I started burning a little bit ago and I cannot pee when I have an infection.

Originally posted by angel451 I checked my urine output after 45 minutes, I got half an ounce out of the 16ounces I drank. How do you still have bladder retention when your taking a water pill? Wonder if I was to avoid drinking anything during these bad episodes, if it would ease up the pain? Then there wouldn't be anything in me to cause all that pressure, at least. I have no idea why, with IC, we can drink tons of water and still only pee a few precious ounces at a time! Where does all that water go (pee pill or not)? But, since there is no answer to this question, it is just one of the mysterious things that IC'ers have to "just accept"--even when it makes absolutely no sense! I will, however, strongly caution you not to avoid drinking liquids as a remedy for the back pain and urethral pressure you are experiencing, because with IC, it is so very important to keep our urine as diluted as possible. If you were to cut down on the amount of water you drink--especially significantly--then you may end up with more and worse bladder pain, in addition to your back and urethral pain! I know how very frustrating all of this can be! And I am so sorry you are suffering from IC too! So, take care Angel--and keep hanging in there! ~Beth

contents: Nice recommendation. LOL. The last 2 days were so horrible, I don't even want to think about it anymore.

I hope you start to feel better! You would think the ER would treat anything, thats what they are there for, emergencies, and extreme pain to me would be an emergency. Do you see a pain doctor if you don't mind me asking? Every now and then I get some referred pain that feels like its my right kidney, but not to often. However I did have my kidneys checked numerous times before being diagnosed with IC, along with lots of other testing to figure out why I always had a UTI, or felt like I did I should say. I did get a few kidney infections in my 20's and I know I had them because I had high fever and pain. I usually know when I have a UTI now. When my symptoms really increase more then a flare, I go have my urine checked just to make sure. But then I didn't know I had IC and was always thinking I had a UTI, therefore I was always at the doctor wondering why nothing was helping my pain and frequency. I hope you feel better. Last edited by lisabar36; 03-15-2009, 01:10 AM.

contents: Some doctors are just jerks I reckon. One told me he'd do a CT on me but nothing would probably show up. I wanted to knock him one. Instead he did an EKG for abdominal pain. Stupid really. I guess they like giving me a rough time. Another let me know plain & clearly that they don't treat chronic pain. No I don't go to a pain specialist. It would be nice to call one on days like that. I pee'd okay today, I didn't feel too bad today. My current plan is to have my bladder checked for a mild prolapse to see if that's why I am having so much trouble urinating and if not, see if I can get the nerves of my bladder checked. I am also going to discuss with my urologist going off the water pill he has me on and doing self catheterization to see if that helps. I drank 65 ounces of liquid the other day and pee only 545 cc (that's less than 8 oz). I cried something awful, it hurt so bad. It's possible the inflammation of my bladder is doing this, because I have had what's felt like 7 UTI's in 1 1/2 mths and only had one treated. My kidney pain is not referred pain from my bladder. It may be due to the retention from my bladder though. I was having really sharp pains in my kidneys the other day while urinating, and I mean kidney stone type sharp pain. It was awful. I also feel urine in my kidneys sometimes. That's not right. I know they are functioning okay but they hurt even when my bladder didn't.

I called to make an appt with my old GYN to see if I could get checked for mild bladder prolapse and got turned down cold. The secretary acted like gynecologists don't do that stuff. A GYN from that clinic did my first surgery. It's probably not even a problem but I would feel better knowing that.

Hi, I am sorry your having problems. I know here urogynecologists I think are the ones that check for prolapses and things like that. Maybe you have one in your area and you can always call and ask first if they can check you for that. I went to a urogyn who also treats IC and he did an extensive exam checking all that as well as checking all my pelvic muscles and pelvic floor. It was the first time I ever had a check up that thorough. After the exam he did let me know everything was ok, no prolapse, all the pain was from the IC, which I kind of new, but it was nice to have such a thorough exam for the first time since I was diagnosed with IC, but I assume because they were a urogyn, and they specialized in female pelvic health, thats why they did that extensive exam on me. So maybe you need to see a urogyn? Its worth calling and asking? I hope you get some answers soon.

contents: This is the only place I can go to a gyn because of insurance issues, unless I drive out of state. And the problem with taking it to my urologist is that I don't completely trust him. I admit I like him, you really can't help but to, but he hasn't been completely honest with me, and so if he was to make this diagnosis, and it turned out nothing was wrong, I wouldn't know whether I should believe him or not. My bladder's been doing alot better over the past few days, I didn't want to say anything cause I was afraid of jinksing it, but the pain/pressure is just mild, I'm peeing really good. So maybe things will keep getting better and I won't even have to wonder why it's happening anymore.

Can someone tell me what edema looks like? I have never had it before. But my legs look somewhat like a purple rash and when I press on my skin, it takes quite a bit of time for the color to fade, is that edema?

I found some videos and pictures and found out what this looks like. I do actually have some pitted edema. I don't need an answer to this now but thanks if you were planning on getting around to it. lol.
Pitting edema can be caused by several things & I would see my family doctor. Feel better soon.

I am posting this on two areas of the message board as I was torn between - - Does this fall under ‘Antibiotics for IC are Controversial’ or ‘Success Stories for IC Patients’. To me, a few days with noticeable improvements w/ no pain is a major success! Has anyone worked with ****? Recently had a consult with her and submitted a broth culture. The first culture was negative… I used betadine on the end of my catheter so the results were tainted. (I know….. not smart). The next culture, I took the test appropriately (did not use betadine on my catheter) and low and behold, had E-Coli and Strep D Enterococcus in my urine. The sensitivity test indicated to take Bactrim for the Strep D. **** advised taking D-Mannose for the E-Coli and Bactrim for the Strep D. I have had a significant reduction in pain for several days!!! I know that antibiotics are controversial, but I have had a suspicion all along that my IC was related to bacteria for two reasons. 1) My IC symptoms began immediately after being diagnosed with bacterial vaginosis. My gyno said my good / bad bacteria were out of wack. 2) I have been sick with a sinus infection twice. When treated with antibiotics, my IC pain subsided for several days. Again, just wanted to see if anyone has had good results with her? I really don’t want to get into the antibiotic debate… I believe IC can be triggered by allergies, diet, bacteria, perhaps other pathogens / diseases (or maybe a combo of all). So I know antibiotics will not work for everyone and I am fully aware that long term antibiotics can be dangerous for your health. Just looking to see if others have had success or worked with her – perhaps we can compare notes. Thanks! Last edited by ICNDonna; 03-11-2009, 02:10 AM. Reason: Removed advertising.

so many drugs! Sunny2 - Are you really on all those drugs at once (in your signature)? How do you know which is working? Doesn't that nurse or naturopath try to get you off of those? Good luck and I hope that you have found the miracle drug for you, antibiotic or not! PV Last edited by ICNDonna; 03-11-2009, 02:10 AM.
signature: I got IC in 1970! I was not diagnosed until 1991. I've tried many drugs and therapies but I tend to only resort to drugs when in a flare because when I am not in a flare (from being good on diet), I suffer only from small bladder volume (like about 7 ozs.) and peeing will relieve the discomfort. When I am feeling relatively normal, I say to myself I am glad I am not on a drug. When I am in a flare, I say, why am I not on a drug! I've recently have been trying to solve my connective issue problems in general. I look to diet and herbs mostly unless it gets really bad. I still think there is great hope for each individual finding a path to healing and there are many.

contents: Antibiotics can help us feel better because they have anti-inflammatory qualities. Nearly all who are helped by elmiron will continue to take it. Donna P.S. Please read the post at http://www.ic-network.com/forum/showthread.php?t=50378 Last edited by ICNDonna; 03-11-2009, 04:06 AM. contents: I haven't posted in a while, but I have been coming to the support forum and reading posts everyday since I was diagnosed. My symptoms came on relatively quickly and I was diagnosed soon after the onset of my symptoms - mostly because I did the research and would not let it go. The onset of my IC symptoms happened in June 2008 and I was diagnosed in August 2008. At my worst, I was going to the bathroom approximately 20 times each day. My symptoms are urethral burning, vague bladder pain at times, urgency, frequency and pelvic floor pain. I found a wonderful Urogyn who truly has the heart of a servant and listens to and spends much time with all his patients. At my first appointment, he spent over 2 hours with me, discussing my treatment options and what to expect from IC. He then scheduled a consultation with a physical therapist for Pelvic floor therapy. I started on Elmiron, Vesicare, the IC diet, lorazepam for relaxing my bladder. UTA when needed and took 7 weeks of pelvic floor therapy, and slowly I started feeling better. It has been about 7 months now and I have many more good days than bad days. I do still have bad days, but those bad days are like my best days used to be. I stick closely to the diet, take my meds as directed, drink approximately 80 ounces of water daily and still practice pelvic floor exercises as needed, and see my doctor as directed and when I need to. However, one of the biggest things I did for myself is that I learned to cope with IC as a life altering condition and I decided to learn to live with it. When I gotta go, I gotta go. Some days that isn't so much, some days it is more. Some days I have urethral burning, some days I don't -- on the days that I do, I pull out the lidocaine ointment and it calms the burning - or I take a UTA and that calms the burning. Some days when my bladder just feels "jumpy" I can take a Tylenol and it will help calm my bladder down. Basically, I am just finding out what works for me and am sticking to it. On average now, I go to the bathroom about every 2 1/2 hours during the day on good days -- bad days, more --and I can sleep about 7 hours without waking up most nights -- I am truly thankful for this progress. The people I work with have been very understanding, and have even given me an office with it's own bathroom. I can be excused from any meeting I am in at any time to go to the restroom -- no questions asked -- I pray daily for remission and I believe that one day it will come to me. I just wanted to post and let everyone know that I am feeling much better and I owe so much of it to this forum -- I have never been one to post much, but I have read and read posts and taken suggestions and tips to heart and have tried many things I have read on here. Having IC has been one of the most humbling experiences of my life and I have become a better person inside because of my experiences with it. I am much more understanding of other people and their illnesses and conditions --I am in a unique situation these days -- I work with mentally ill patients, and I am constantly seeing people who are in much worse condition that I ever thought about being -- this keeps me focused on life and not on IC -- I pray daily for a cure for IC and for those of us suffering from this illness -- I hope each of you feels better day by day.

contents: hi everyone, i have struggled with bladder pain for 2 years now. this site has helped me so much and i thank everyone for your support. i won't go too into my story. my symptoms are mostly pain and frequency. after seeing several different uros, there was a lot of speculation that i had IC, but i never got a firm diagnosis. i don't think i have IC (i dunno what i have), but i certainly identify with a lot of your stories. anyway, i was in tremendous pain last summer and desperately searching this website and i found an excerpt from a book called "A Headache in the Pelvis" by Dr. David Wise and Dr. Rodney Anderson. while reading the post, i intuitively felt like this is what was going on for me and so i immediately ordered the book. since the day i read it and started trying some of their suggestions, i have been feeling 95% better. i went from daily intense pain to living a normal life. i still have some pain sometimes and it is certainly a gradual process, but i'm doing much much better. the book suggests that pelvic pain has a lot to do with tensing the pelvic muscles. i recommend reading their work/protocol, but i can say that just becoming aware that i've been holding tension in my pelvis (and then making an effort to relax and let it be, instead of getting all worked up about it) has made a significant difference to me. i started doing this in sept of last year and have been wanting to post a success story on this website, but also wanting to wait for a few months to see if i really was getting better. i'm also starting to get physical therapy, so i'm hoping that'll help even more. anyway, i just wanted to share how much this book and their protocol has helped me. they talk about all different kinds of pelvic pain, including IC. i am so grateful that i was introduced to this book through this website. i wish you all well and hope you are able to heal from your pain. take care. contents: I'm not looking for that, I would like to have something to help me pee better and relax my bladder. It's so shy, I go to the bathroom anywhere in public, and it takes a really long time before I can go. Even if someone is in the next room, my bladder doesn't like it. I could care less but hey that doesn't seem to matter. I also have trouble peeing, not all of the time, but when it happens, I don't like it.

I have been wondering about that book. How much was it? I suppose I could look up the cost myself. I think I mainly have PFD, also. Maybe a little IC, but I think it is caused by the PFD because foods usually don't affect my frequency or pain. They are usually constant. But warm baths and xanax do help. Dr. Wise called me today because I submitted a request for info on his website. But their program is $4,000. That's too much for me right now. But I start PT on Tuesday, so I think it will help. I have slowly but surely been feeling better by doing two hot baths per day. I put Aveeno in my bath now so that I don't get irritated by all the baths. And I use flushable moist wipes so I don't get irritated skin down there from all the wiping from all the urinating. Anyway, I'd be very interested to hear what has helped you from that book. I also am trying to be aware of relaxing the pelvic floor as much as possible. It does help. And the squatting stretch I think helps a little.

contents: angel451, the book doesn't mention anything specifically about a shy bladder, but i still think it's worth a read. musiclover, i also PMed you, but i have found stopping the anxiety-tension-pain cycle to be the most helpful. and donna, i don't believe the book can heal you, but i do truly believe that SOME pple can completely heal from pelvic pain by using techniques such as those suggested in this book. and at the very least, it can help a lot. there's a lot of wisdom in the book and the techniques are not doing any harm.

Hi everyone! I just wanted to share what great success I've been having with Elival. Aside from a little bit of blurred vision and dry mouth in the mornings, it has done wonders for my bladder. The constant pressure I had day and night has almost completely disappeared. I can now get a good nights sleep, only getting up once or twice to use the washroom (but I go right back to sleep afterwards) and am generally able to enjoy life again. I can socialize, entertain at my home, go shopping with my girlfriends, and enjoy dinner and a movie with my husband. A complete turn around from 1 month ago. I hope that everyone has had the opportunity to try this medication (or something like it) and can enjoy some of the benfits it brings to many IC patients. Without this support network, I never would have known to request Elival from my Urologist (who seems to know less about IC than I do!). So, thank you to everyone who urged me to fight for my health and explore other options. You all are wonderful!!

contents: So, I'll be brief. I'm just really happy right now, so I wanted to share this with other people who can understand this. And hopefully a lot of you are also experiencing similar "success". I found out Friday that I probably have IC (I'm getting the full examination, test, etc. next week). But the doctor is fairly convinced. I simply described my "symptoms", actually just how I've felt for my entire life, and she looked at me and said "oh, you probably have interstitial cystitis. unfortunately, we could have fixed this when you were a kid, if you'd gone to a doctor about this. fortunately, we can still fix it now." Okay. So, after my research, I'm anticipating months of eating/drinking super carefully, drugs, god knows what else before I'm feeling better. No. It's been 3 days. Friday was the last day I had coffee (and I had a lot to give it a proper farewell). I used the bathroom 29 times within 24 hours. Saturday, 22 times. Yesterday, 8. INCONCEIVABLE. Never in my entire LIFE have I been able to say I went the bathroom 8 times in 24 hours. In 23 years. And today, I'm feeling quite well, I'm on the same track. I'll shoot for 10 and see what happens. I still have some general discomfort, I don't feel perfect. But hey, all I've done is cut out coffee, alcohol, juice, citrus, etc. for a couple days. Seriously. And I already feel 100 times better. So, I'm a little peeved that I could have learned this 20 years ago. But, I'm also pretty happy that I'll know this for the next 60.....

Oh also Part of why I've perhaps never noticed what a difference my diet would make is that I've been drinking coffee and tea since I was 8 or 9. Actually, I was probably drinking tea before that. And I had orange pineapple juice with breakfast, daily. And tons of pizza and spaghetti sauce... Do you see where I'm going with this? Yeah. I haven't gone 2 days straight without coffee in at least a decade. Crazy.
That's great that you've already found such a simple way to reduce your symptoms. Awesome! I hope it continues to get better and better!

contents: I am not free of IC but I am experiencing longer remissions. 4 days in a row sometimes. I remaiin having difficulty with various things such as riding in public transportation and cars, sitting too long, tiring and other IC symptoms. During those difficult painful pressure moments, what helps is to realize things are impermanent. I have never had more than a day or two of comfort before. It really helps to be on disability and nap as often as necessary during the day and listen to relazation CDs and calm music in bed.Also short walks. For me, a big help is finding the right homeopathic practitioner. And diet which goes beyond not eating citris etc. Everyone is different but for me, once I cut out processed sugar and meat to eat raw fruit and veggies and raw protein for 6 years now it helps.Plus I shed over 55 lbs of fat. I intend to continue this way of eatiing. Cooked vegeterian food once iin a while is ok but easy on the dairy. I don't overdo protein. The World Health Organization says people eat much too much protein and I read here about this some years ago. Also helping are anti spasmodic meds and honnoring my limitations. Instead of getting frustrated and angry, I am gentle wiith myself. I hope this is the beginning of a new trend but it takes patience. All installations etc. only made things very much worse, everyone is different. To all here God bless you and never give up no matter what is going on. The doctor also prescribed pain meds which I take when necessary. If I don't wait too long I can get by on less. God bless all reading this with new hope. From someone with 17 years of IC. Sure it can get bad and flare but I am heere to bring new hope to those sreading this. Sorry if I am a "wordy birdie".

Dear IC Sufferers: I would like to tell you the whole sordid saga of my IC battle but it would take up this whole web site and bore you to tears. So, the bottom line for me is this: NO MORE YEAST/MOLD/FUNGI FOODS! Problem solved - after years of many, many pain-filled days, useless expensive medications, lung surgery and being home-bound with no social life. Last October, after eating a Portobello sandwich, I had the worst flare (I call it an “attack“) I have ever had. When I recovered from that, I was pain-free for a month afterwards. Was this a clue? Did I receive an inoculation from those mushrooms? You can believe I haven’t touched a mushroom since! After that wonderful month of pain-free bliss, small flares started creeping back up. They were tolerable at first but gradually increased until I thought I was back to square one. I really wasn’t brave enough to try for another "Portobello vaccination" so I started researching fungi and mold in foods which led me to yeast in foods. VOILA!! ENLIGHTENING STRIKE!! (Pun intended.) Well, bread is a no-brainer and I am a breadoholic. The mushrooms gave themselves away when they triggered the “attack” and blue cheese is obvious, but aged cheese can also be a trigger. Beer is a given, but wine? Yeast comes from the grape skins. (Even before I knew this, I had flares after eating red grapes, so I had already given them up and red wine is worse than white.) Miso soup, soy products (tofu), tempeh? No more vitamin B-complex either. I was surprised to find out how many foods are processed with mold. The list goes on. It takes a lot longer to shop now. Actually, I have been checking labels for years but now I have to look for yeast and soy ingredients as well. No problem - I can do that! I am off the Ditropan but I still take a probiotic and fiber caps every day (I have found that keeping the colon cleansed helps alleviate pressure on the bladder) . I also take Claritin now that it is OTC. I still have some frequency and some urgency and occasional discomfort, but nothing like the debilitating pain I had experienced for months on end. I have been pain-free again for four weeks now. I believe that, because IC has no defined etiology, it is a symptom. I am certain that mold or fungi or yeast are not the cause of everyone’s IC but I do believe that mold is the cause of my IC and the damage that has been done to my bladder may never be healed. Hopefully I will be able to prevent any further damage. My favorite saying is: “If God leads you to it, He’ll lead you through it”. I have read many of your posts and they have helped me a lot. I didn’t join this site or comment before because I didn’t have anything to offer until now. God helped me through my ordeal and hopefully this post, through Him, will help someone else. God bless you all!
Thank you for sharing. Interestingly nearly all the foods you mentioned are on the problem list on the IC diet. I'm glad you are doing well. Donna

contents: I started on a month long course of chinese herbal therapy a week ago, and I am feeling much better already. Different therapist have mentioned weak kidneys, others mentioned a weakened heart. Western medicine-wise I am doing great, my blood tests came back superb, and so did my creatinine and IVP results. But I am told I could still be sick, oriental medicine speaking. I am at the beginning of what could be a long road of imbalance and chronic illness. In addition, I started on long term atb tx a week ago also, so I am not sure which one of the Txs I owe this success to. I am feeling 90+% better and I can already tolerate a few more foods now. I am feeling cautious though since it's too early to tell. I am sleeping through the night tho, I can hold a lot more pee in my bladder. I am making much faster progress than when I was taking Uva Ursi tea alone. I understand long term atb tx is very controversial, and scary. But my urologist and I agreed that we are both willing to give it a blind-but informed stab at it because it has helped quite a few ICers stay free of Sx's for many years. Some will say IC is not an infection. Some will say that if someone got 100% better with atbs and atfs then this person never had IC to begin with. I really don't know what caused my IC, and I don't claim to know, I have my hunches for now, and I may never find out for sure. IC may have many components and layers as to its cause, could be neurological, psychological, psychosocial, immunological, nutritional (deficiency), toxicological, etc, etc. But I do know this for sure however: I was given the official diagnosis of IC by my urologist, he firmly believes my signs and symptoms based on many procedures and labs, that I have all the classic symptoms of IC, including polyps (which does not occur in everyone with IC). Was my uro mistaken? that is a possibility, since doctors don't know it all. But if this is the case here, then such could be the case with multitudes of other urologists around the country and the world for that matter that are mistakenly diagnosing people with IC that could be helped with these "controversial treatments" which could never help people with real IC. will the real IC please stand up? What is true IC? Can anyone in this forum say with absolute certainty that this illness could never have a microbial aspect to it? I am not a big proponent of creating atb resistant pathogens in our bodies. but that could just be what we could already have and have been having for many years by treating a multitude of Sx's with the wrong overprescribed Rxs over the years. In addition to the controversial cause being something that can also lurk under the radar and be hard to detect. When you have a wound, that wound needs to be free of infection and other irritating compounds for it to completely heal, the infection/chemical/thing may or may not have caused the wound, but it sure could be stopping or slowing any sort of progress or healing. I don't want to stir controversy over this issue. I just want people to get better, if there is a way for them to get better. real IC or not should not be the point. The point should be more about having a diagnosis of IC and not limiting possibilities of finding an answer to this problem. thanks everyone, this forum is great. contents: I want to take and yell to the world....I DON"T LEAK ANYMORE!!! :woohoo: Ok, let me start from the beginning. I'm kind of stupid becuse for at least the last 2 years I thought I had "fluid" coming down from the "V" area. It itched all the time, had odor and it had been getting worse and worse. Well, yestrday was the first day to try the Detrol LA. Nothing came from that area the whole day so I started thinking it must have been the bladder leaking all these years. The reason I thought it had to be the "V" area was I was told you could only have a leaking bladder from having a baby, surgery or an accident which I had't. Well, this morning I had a little leaking but I was doing a lot of lifting but other wise NOT ONE LEAK ALL DAY!! :woohoo: I even put to the test. I stood and folded two loads of laundy. I kept talking to things down there (in my mind of course...not out loud.) come on... do something. I've been standig long enough now do something or forever hold your piece. I waited and waited for some kind of leaking and nothing!!! :woohoo: It seems so strange too. It's like I've been freed from panty liners. Only thing with the Detral LA I have had a migraine since around 3:00 am this morning and it makes me very sleepy. Plus my mouth and throat get dry. I take it with lunch and around 3:00 pm through evening I sort of sound like a husky mountian man. Anyway, I very seldom have anything good to post about and heaven knows how little good happens with pills for me but I'm hoping I'm not jinxing it by posting about this. I still in my mind am thinking something will go wrong or it will all go back to leaking again. I just wonder how I eneded up with a leaking bladder when I never had a baby?????????
Having PFD problems will cause you to leak.. I am SO glad you found something to help you!! :woohoo:

contents: Hi sweetie! I am so glad you have found something that works for you! I meant to post on your other thread the other day, but fell asleep before I posted. But, I also wanted to tell you that I am so glad that you went back to your Uro. From the things you have written about him over the years, he seems to be a good person and a good Doctor, and also seems to truly care about his patients. I know you guys had a rough patch there and one particularly bad visit. But, please remember that anyone can have an off day, even Doctors! I am just happy that you thought it over and decided to give him another chance, and that he reciprocated and did the same for you. After all, he knows your history better than anyone else and as such, he is in the best position to be able to help you both short term and long term. There is one other thing I wanted to mention to you though....regarding your success with Detrol and the leaking. Since you arent certain if the leaking has been coming from your vagina or from your bladder, I urge you to have the fluid tested. I say that because from what you described, it is quite possible that it could be BV (bacterial vaginosis). BV can cause the exact same symptoms you described and can be cured with a simple course of antibiotics. Also, the itching doesnt seem consistant with leaking urine. Not only that, but I think you would have been certain if it had been urine leaking. The drying meds, such as Detrol, would also "dry up" bacterial vaginios symptoms, in effect, making you think you were curing a leaking bladder, when in fact you were only getting symptomatic relief from BV. Your Uro can do a simple test in his office to check the fluid to see if it is BV or leaking urine. Since you havent been certain of the orgin of it, I urge you to make an appt and get this confirmation. BV can be very serious if it continues without treatment including (but not limited to): Pelvic Inflammatory Disease (PID), (which can often mimic endometrios as well IC symptoms), infertility, damage to fallopian tubes, internal scarring of organs and other tissue often leading to Pelvic Adhesions, neurological (nerve) damage, and many other problems and or diseases too numerous to name! Left untreated long enough, it can even cause heart disease! I urge you to get checked out and make sure that the Detrol is drying up urine and not liquid from Bacterial Vaginiosis. Many, many women have thought they had leaking bladders only to discover years later it was BV all along, after thay have already done permenant damage. If upon examination your Doctor confirms that the liquid IS in fact urine, and you decide to continue taking Detrol, but are bothered by the side effects of dry mouth and throat, you might want to ask your Dr. to r/x a med such as artificial salava, a r/x oral rinse, or a med that helps you produce more. Sucking on sugar free candy or chewing sugar free gum can also help you produce more salava. Dry mouth is not only an irritating side effect, but it can also cause sore throats as well major dental problems such as gum disease, gingivitis, cavities, and many other things. So please let your Dr. know about this and ask for some help with it if you decide to stay on Detrol or any other of the drying type of meds. As for the migraine you experianced, it may have just been coincidental. If it happens again, it might help to take the med with some food or a meal. If it still happens, let your Dr. know. He may want to swith you to one of the other meds in this class. But, PLEASE get this fluid checked to confirm it's origin!!!! Your health is so important, and YOU are so important to all of us! Sending my hugs and love, Amaranthe

contents: Good points, Briza! Also, I didnt realize they had the home kits to test for this like they do UTIs! That is great to know since I have had BV and it is notorious for coming back. But, I did want to mention that with mine, I had symtoms for a long time, but I didnt have the extreme itching that many ppl get. I did itch occasionally, but mine definately wasnt extreme, even though the BV had gone undiagnosed for a very long time. I also had Zero burning. That's why it never occured to me that mine could be BV. I just went to the Dr. for a routine visit for a yeast infection, when he d/xed me with it. I DID have a yeast infection too, but during the exam, he noticed the watery fluid, and mentioned it, and I told him it was just my "normal discharge". (Even though I had a total hysterectomy, I thought this was just normal and every woman had this type of discharge. (That's how naive I was!) But, nope, turned out it was BV, even though I had no other symptoms with it! So, PLEASE Mary, definately check this out to make sure that yours isnt too, since as ICers, we often dont have "classic symptoms" of anything. We are an odd lot, when it comes to our bodies, and sometimes when we have things wrong, they dont present themselves the same way healthy ppls issues do. BUt, again, Bri, thank you for your post! You had some great information that I know I will definately be using! Also, you are right that with urine repeatedly leaking or sitting there before someone has the chance to change(like if it happens while you are away from home), it could defiinately cause itching too, (similar to the way a diaper rash would.) Thanks again, Bri! Love and hugs to both of you, amaranthe

contents: Oh! I almost forgot! I also meant to tell you to be sure to increase your water consumption and stay on top of Constipation while taking Detrol (or any of the drying meds). After all, the same mechanism in these meds that dries out the bladder also dries out bowel movements, frequently causing constipation. So, it might not be a bad idea to get some OTC Glyceran suppositories to use prior to bowel movements while on this med. (They are very gentle. They arent stimulants and dont make have a BM, but rather they help to soften them up for when you go on your own. They are very inexpensive and most pharmacies carry them. They also are not addictive. But, the most important thing is to increase your liquid volume. (This will also help with the dry mouth.)

contents: Hi, I wanted to share some exciting news! I had an ELISA (Enzyme-Linked ImmunoSorbent Assay)test done. It's a blood test. The level of what are called "IgG antibodies" are measured, and if particularly high levels of IgG antibodies are found when testing a certain food, it means that the patient is reacting against that food and should probably try to avoid it. My doctor said that the body treats these foods like viruses or foreign bodies and continuously tries to attack them, thus creating inflammation. Anyway, I have elminated what showed up (chicken & eggs, which I ate a lot of.....and milk, celery and aspargus...how weird!). I started elimnating those foods on December 8th and have not has any IC symptoms since!!!! I even quit taking the Elmiron and I had a glass of wine the other night. I'm not suggesting this is the answer for everyone, but it has done wonders for me.
Donna

contents: This is so great. Congratulations. Take care of yourself.

Wanted to give everyone out there some hope! Have been taking heparin, sodium bicarb, lidocaine, kenalog bladder instills and feel great! :woohoo: To those worried that their pain will not subside, please hang in there. I was diagnosed a few months ago. Thought life was over... Began taking Elmiron and hydroxyzine in December and Cystoprotek in January. Also, learned to cook. -- Invested in a steamer and must say veggies with a lot of butter are not so bad! Began bladder instills a couple of months ago and as I mentioned, seeing some real progress. My pain level the past two days has been zero! Also, changing positions at work (less stress) has probably helped. So please don't lose hope. I was ready to throw in the towel a while back. Chin up and stay positive! - - Oh, and don’t forget to pray. It has helped to know that God is with me, guiding me through this journey!
:woohoo: back at you!!!! I am so glad you are getting some relief! Sounds like you have a very good doctor.... Hugs!

contents: I've totally kicked my bladder pain with birth control and Atarax (think it's really the former that helps, but I'm not sure). Still not sure if I have IC or endo, or both. Anyway, with this combo, bladder pain is gone. Anyway, I'm CONVINCED that my urethral pain (my only remaining symptom) is a side effect of my medications--Atarax and/or birth control--not a part of IC, per se--as I lived with severe bladder pain for a year WITHOUT any urethral pain. I had sex, no problem at all. When I started on Atarax and bc, the urethral pain started almost rightr away. When I touched my urethra and especially above it, it was extremely dry and painful. Then I started using Estrace--estrogen cream--on the urethra. It helps tremendouly when I use it. However, my doctor says this doesn't make sense--she's never heard of somenone's urethra being helped by Estrace. She's now given me neurontin (see my post in newly diagnosed for more, as desired), just becauise she thinks it'll be even more effective. However, I think any lack of efficacy of the Estarce is just due to me not putting it on consistently sometimes. Does anyone have a similar story? Has Estrace helped anyone with urethral pain?

Neurontin & Lignocaine Hi there, Neurontin & Lignocaine have helped me for urethral and above the urethra (clitoral) pain. How are you doing with the Neurontin? It helped me for years and lots of other people I know too. I found it especially good for neuropathic pins & needles, stinging & burning. Regards Shelley

Thanks so much for the post, Shelley. I've just started on Neurontin, so I can't tell quite yet; at least my uro said it should take a month to work (seems a bit long--is this right)? A few questions, if you don't mind: -Were you on topical and the pill-form of Neurontin (I'm on the latter)? -If you took pills, did you have any weight gain or edema? I'm really not thrilled about this potential side effect -Did Neurontin stop working for you, or were you able to go off it besides your nerves calmed down/why are you not on it now? Thanks so much!!

PS-I tried lidocaine, and it didn't help at all: for some reason, it made me worse. This is why I think my issue is different than most IC'ers--it seems more hormonally-based (caused by side effects of Atarax birth control/helped by Estrace) than nerve-based. If the nerve medicine does help, I still feel like I'm just covering up the issue, so to speak--the dryness and hormonal issue. This is just my theory.

Neurontin What dose of Neurontin are you taking? I needed 300mgs 2x/day but I have a friend who finds that 100mgs 2x/day works for her. We both noticed an improvement in symptoms within a few days. Also I started off cautiously with 100mgs 2x/day and slowly increased dose as needed. I never had any side affects from Neurontin tablets and didn't know it came in topical form. It did stop working for me after about 5 years, but this is because I had a High Positive Ureaplasma Infection that was continuing to get worse over 16 years. I was just diagnosed this year and have finally re-tested Negative after taking a month course of Doxycycline. I changed from Neurontin to Lyrica which is working better than Neurontin for pain. I'm only feeling slightly better with my Infection cleared and am hoping that 16 years of infection has not done permanent damage. I hope this helps. Regards Shelley
Estrace cream not only keeps your bladder and urethra healthy, but also prevents and/or cures vaginal atrophy.

estrace is great I can't believe your doctor said she's never heard of Estrace helping with urethral burning or pain...It's pretty widely accepted nowadays that the whole urinary tract has lots of estrogen receptors and therefore is very responsive to changes in estrogen levels. 15 years ago when I had my first real bout of IC (it coincided with an early menopause for me) and they said they were giving me estrogen cream for dryness and some of menopausal symptoms...but the thing I found out on my own was that the estrace worked wonders on my bladder, especially when it was supplemented by oral estrogen (now I use an estrogen patch). I know there are those who say that they do worse with estrogen but for me it's been a lifesaver!

Thanks so much for confirming, all! It sounds like it might be time for a new doctor. I'm taking 100mg three times a day. So far, I can't tell any difference--and I'm actually peeing a lot more than usual (with a lot of volume).

estrace was a life saver I'm not exaggerating when I say estrace was a life saver for me, I was diagnosed with severe IC (Hunner's ulcer) through bladder dilation. The first urologist I saw was a much older man who told me hormones have an enormous impact on the bladder, but he sent me to a another urologist who was supposed to be 'brilliant'. The 'brilliant' urologist got annoyed when I mentioned the word hormone. Since I was having hormonal problems I saw another 3 urologists, all of whom told me that IC had nothing to do with hormones. Luckily, my GP is open minded (I am not in menopause) and prescribed Estrace for me, and what do you know? The pain and urgency in my bladder went from 10/10 (I literally had NO LIFE) to about 2/10. Still having some sensitivity in the urethra, but am about to try Vagifem (we can't get Estrace cream in Canada, I've been taking it by pill). If it wasn't for my GP being willing to listen to me, my life would still be a living hell. I would recommend to any woman who is having any symptoms of a hormonal imbalance to pursue hormonal treatments, whether or not you're in menopause, whether or not your estradiol levels are 'normal'. You know your own body best. I wish I could do something to thank my GP other than saying thank-you!!
